chunk_summary: Guidance on GLP-1 therapy in India, focusing on access, cost, and cold-chain counseling for semaglutide, including Wegovy and upcoming generics.
learning_objectives: Understand the evolving landscape of GLP-1-based anti-obesity pharmacotherapy in India.
Learn counseling strategies for affordability, supply continuity, and cold-chain management.
Familiarize with specific storage and handling guidelines for semaglutide products like Wegovy.
Develop contingency plans for missed doses due to supply or storage issues.
key_facts: Wegovy (semaglutide) launched in India in June 2025 for chronic weight management.
Sun Pharma received DCGI approval in January 2026 to manufacture generic semaglutide post-patent expiry.
Wegovy pens require refrigeration but can be stored at room temperature for up to 28 days within specified limits.
Access counseling should address affordability, supply continuity, and cold-chain reliability.
Families should use insulated pouches for transport and sharps containers for disposal.
reference_quote: Sun Pharma published that it received DCGI approval to manufacture and market a generic semaglutide injection for chronic weight management and plans launch after semaglutide patent expiry in India.
document_key_topics: GLP-1 therapy access and cost in India
Cold-chain and storage counseling for semaglutide
Affordability and supply continuity in obesity management
chunk_heading: OVERNUTRITION & OBESITY MANAGEMENT;PHARMACOTHERAPY AND BARIATRIC SURGERY ESCALATION;INDIA- SPECIFIC ACCESS, COST, AND COLD-CHAIN COUNSELING FOR GLP-1 THERAPY;
